title: Use the Distributive Property
essential_question: How can you use the Distributive Property to solve multiplication problems?
strategies: Break the larger factor into tens: 6 × 30 = 6 × (10 + 10 + 10) = 60 + 60 + 60 = 180.
Always check your answer with basic facts.

pretest.stem: Norma practices violin for 50 minutes every day. How many minutes does she practice in 7 days?
A backsplash needs 5 rows of 20 tiles. How many tiles are needed?
Each snack pack holds 20 crackers. How many crackers in 4 packs?
A machine makes 70 springs each hour. How many springs in 8 hours?

learn.title: The Distributive Property
learn.content: You can break apart one factor to make the multiplication easier. Example: 6 × 30 = 6 × (10 + 10 + 10) = 60 + 60 + 60 = 180.

learn.title: Using Place Value
learn.content: Think of 30 as 3 tens. So 6 × 30 = 6 × 3 tens = 18 tens = 180. This works for any multiple of 10.

learn.title: Solving Word Problems
learn.content: Read the problem, identify the two factors, and use the Distributive Property if one factor is a multiple of 10. Example: 8 buses × 40 students = 8 × 4 tens = 32 tens = 320 students.

hints: Think about what use distributive property means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how use distributive property works.
